Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show P9 overexpression increased neophytadiene (8.4-fold in tomato), reduced chlorophyll by 54%, and increased whitefly preference; authors link this to chlorophyll degradation and attraction.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Section 2.6 states P9 is unique to Crinivirus; Discussion states P9 is a putative 9 kDa protein encoded by RNA2.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Neophytadiene was measured from infected/overexpressing tomato leaves/plants; the paper context is leaf volatile production.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Experimental section specifies tomato cv. Micro-Tom.

Source_Species.reason: P9 is explicitly a protein of tomato chlorosis virus (ToCV).

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results 2.1 and 2.7 show neophytadiene attracted B. tabaci and BtOBP2 silencing reduced ToCV accumulation after transmission, supporting a role in facilitating transmission.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results and methods describe ToCV infection and transient overexpression of viral proteins in tobacco and tomato.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper suggests new avenues for managing virus disease, but does not explicitly state P9 is a target for antiviral engineering.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Neophytadiene is called a specialized metabolite/volatile, but the exact classification 'diterpenoid hydrocarbon' is not explicitly stated in the paper text.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Y2H, Co-IP, luciferase complementation, BiFC, and co-localization support interaction with Lhca4.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: P9 expression caused chlorophyll loss and chloroplast ultrastructural damage; reduced photosynthetic performance is not directly measured but is reasonably implied by PSI/PSII disruption and chloroplast damage.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three listed findings are directly supported by Results sections 2.3, 2.4, and 2.6, including 8.4-fold neophytadiene increase and 54% chlorophyll reduction.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Experimental section identifies tomato as Solanum lycopersicum Mill. cv. Micro-Tom.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state ToCV genes were cloned into PVX pGR106 for transient expression; interaction assays used 1300-35S-derived vectors such as 1300-GFP and 1300-35S-3*FLAG.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper consistently names the virus as tomato chlorosis virus; no alternative source species is involved for P9.

Omics_Data.reason: Results 2.6 describe comparative proteomics and methods specify TMT LC-MS/MS; no accession is given in the paper, matching 'accession NA'.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Results 2.4 state transiently overexpressed P9 colocalized with Lhca4 in both cytoplasm and nuclei.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in Results/methods: transient overexpression, metabolite profiling, preference assays, Y2H, Co-IP, luciferase complementation, BiFC, co-localization, BN-PAGE, and comparative proteomics.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper shows P9 acts in tomato and tobacco and links chlorophyll degradation/neophytadiene to whitefly behavior.

Applied_Species.reason: Tomato is the main applied host species for the core P9 functional assays.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: P9 is a viral protein encoded by ToCV and used in overexpression assays, but the paper does not directly profile endogenous P9 expression during infection.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The metabolite linked to P9 is explicitly neophytadiene.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show Lhca4 overexpression increased chlorophyll, reduced neophytadiene, reduced whitefly preference, and mitigated ToCV effects.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results 2.4 and 2.5 identify Lhca4 as an LHC I subunit/photosystem I antenna protein.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Neophytadiene was quantified from tomato plants/leaves, including OELhca4 plants.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify Solanum lycopersicum cv. Micro-Tom.

Source_Species.reason: Lhca4 is a tomato host gene throughout the study.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Neophytadiene was shown to attract whiteflies and BtOBP2 silencing reduced virus transmission, supporting a role in promoting virus spread.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results include ToCV infection, P9 expression, transgenic overexpression, and CRISPR knockout of Lhca4.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The data imply utility for engineering resistant/less attractive lines, but the paper does not explicitly present Lhca4 in these exact applied terms.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Paper identifies neophytadiene as a specialized metabolite/volatile but does not explicitly call it a diterpenoid hydrocarbon.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Physical interaction between Lhca4 and P9 is demonstrated by multiple assays.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results/Supporting Information state Lhca4 knockout shortened growth period and caused stunted seeds; overexpression changed height and chlorophyll traits.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements are directly supported by Results 2.4-2.6 and Supporting Information for knockout phenotypes.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods give Solanum lycopersicum Mill. cv. Micro-Tom.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The pBWA(V)HS-osgfp vector is explicitly stated for overexpression, and 1300-based constructs are used in transient assays, but the exact promoter composition for all listed constructs is not fully specified in the paper text.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Lhca4 is from tomato, identified as Solanum lycopersicum in methods.

Omics_Data.reason: Comparative proteomics by TMT LC-MS/MS is described; no accession is provided, so 'accession NA' is consistent.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results 2.6 explicitly list Lhca4 as Q7M1K8 in the proteomics context.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Results 2.4 state P9 and Lhca4 colocalize in both cytoplasm and nuclei.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Tomato source plants in this study are Micro-Tom; Lhca4 transgenic lines were generated in this cultivar.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are reported for Lhca4: overexpression, CRISPR knockout, qRT-PCR, chlorophyll and neophytadiene assays, whitefly preference assays, Y2H, Co-IP, LCI, BiFC, and co-localization.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results show ToCV suppressed Lhca4, OELhca4 induced CHLH/CHLM/POR, and altered PAO/PPH/RCCR responses under infection.

Applied_Species.reason: The applied species for Lhca4 functional assays is tomato.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results 2.5 explicitly state ToCV infection suppressed Lhca4 expression.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The metabolite linked to Lhca4 function is neophytadiene.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Lhca4 expression was reduced by ToCV infection and elevated in overexpression lines (5.8-fold increase).

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results 2.7 show BtOBP2 binds neophytadiene, silencing abolishes preference for neophytadiene, and reduces ToCV accumulation after vector-mediated transmission.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: BtOBP2 is explicitly described as an odorant-binding protein.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Neophytadiene is part of the tomato plant volatile blend emitted from leaves/plants and tested as a host cue.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Neophytadiene attracted whiteflies and BtOBP2-mediated perception affected virus transmission outcomes.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results and methods include neophytadiene fumigation/exposure, dsRNA silencing, and transmission assays.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper implies management relevance but does not explicitly frame BtOBP2 as a vector-interference target in engineering terms.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Paper does not explicitly state neophytadiene is a diterpenoid hydrocarbon, only that it is a volatile/specialized metabolite.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Neophytadiene is a metabolite, not a protein; the field label requests interacting proteins, so this value is mismatched.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements are directly supported: induction over time, strongest affinity with Ki 2.02 μM, and silencing abolished preference and reduced ToCV accumulation.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods identify the whitefly as Bemisia tabaci.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: BtOBP2 source species is Bemisia tabaci.

Omics_Data.reason: Results/methods describe AlphaFold2 modeling and molecular docking; no accession is given, so 'accession NA' is acceptable.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Discussion explicitly describes OBPs as small soluble proteins in the sensillar lymph of insect antennae and other olfactory organs, supporting a soluble extracellular olfactory localization.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The whitefly colony used is MED.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in Results/methods for BtOBP2.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results state BtOBP3 also increased transcriptionally, but BtOBP3 silencing did not abolish neophytadiene preference, supporting BtOBP2 as the key OBP.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results 2.7 show BtOBP2 expression increased over 0-96 h under neophytadiene exposure.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The metabolite interacting with BtOBP2 is neophytadiene.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same as above; BtOBP2 expression is induced by neophytadiene exposure.

corrections.reason: Neophytadiene is a metabolite, not a protein; the field label requests interacting proteins, so this value is mismatched.
